description: BF16 instead of 4-bit - avoids dequantization overhead
vLLM - install failed (numba incompatible with Python 3.13)
torch.compile on BF16 Unsloth - marginal gain not worth complexity
FastLanguageModel + Qwen3-4B (pure text) - no vision overhead
Qwen3-0.6B - smaller model, less memory bandwidth per token
greedy decoding - barely faster, not the bottleneck
custom loop - Unsloth needs explicit position_ids (fixed next)
custom loop - Unsloth returns tuple not CausalLMOutputWithPast (fixed next)
custom loop with return_dict=True + position_ids - slight improvement
batched inference batch_size=8 - 8.5x gain amortizing per-step overhead
batch_size=16 - near-linear scaling, same step time as batch=8
batch_size=32 - 1.96x more than batch=16, still near-linear
batch_size=64 - 1.97x more than batch=32, linear scaling continues
batch_size=128 - 1.99x more than batch=64, still near-linear
batch_size=256 - OOM at ~37GB (model 1.2GB + KV cache overflow)
batch_size=192 - OOM at ~39GB near end of generation
